trigger.reason: US government ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 model releases is a major substance event from a top-tier lab with high market attention potential across AI-adjacent sectors.

primary_event.headline: US government bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 releases over security concerns
primary_event.summary: The US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to bypass Fable 5's guardrails. Anthropic contends similar jailbreaks exist in competing models. Cybersecurity researchers have signed an open letter opposing the ban.

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed the US government ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models as a sympathy-fade trigger across enterprise software, cybersecurity, and AI infrastructure adjacencies.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger is a regulatory action against Anthropic specifically, not a capability release that displaces vendor revenue — so the sympathy-fade logic is particularly weak. Semi-cap names (PLAB, AMBA, AEIS, ONTO) actually traded UP today, suggesting the market reads the ban as bullish for the picks-and-shovels trade. The clean unjustified downside reactions are concentrated in vertical-workflow SaaS (VRRM, NCNO, EVH, TYL), where the panic narrative (AI eats workflow software) is mechanically disconnected from a regulatory ban on Anthropic models.

discoveries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K acknowledges AI competition (self-driving vehicles, AI-enabled tools) as a general risk but emphasizes municipal contracts, ballot-initiative-protected enforcement frameworks, physical camera infrastructure, and a new 5-year NYCDOT contract effective Jan 2026. The 10-Q flags Commercial Services contract renewal risk as the actual near-term overhang — unrelated to AI.
discoveries.thesis: An Anthropic model ban has zero mechanical bearing on automated photo enforcement of red-light, speed, and bus-lane violations. The product is a regulated physical-evidence chain with multi-year municipal contracts; LLMs don't issue tickets or appear in traffic court. The real Verra risk factor is Commercial Services renewal pricing, which is a 2026 contract negotiation issue, not an AI displacement story.
discoveries.what_confirms: Renewal of the under-negotiation Commercial Services contract on reasonable terms; continued NYCDOT execution; no further Ontario-style legislative bans.
discoveries.what_kills: More jurisdictions ban automated enforcement (Ontario precedent), or the in-negotiation Commercial Services customer walks. These are real risks but UNRELATED to today's trigger.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Physical-infrastructure + regulated-evidence businesses get caught in software sympathy fades despite having no software-replacement risk. Track the basket of camera/enforcement/tolling names on every AI-lab news day.
discoveries.catalyst: US government bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 releases over security concerns
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Verra is on AI-adjacent SaaS lists due to its 'smart mobility technology' positioning, making it an automatic sympathy-fade target.
research_pointers: Status of Commercial Services contract renewal currently in negotiation (10-Q flag)
Pace of new NYCDOT 5-year contract execution and any service-level credits accrued
Other jurisdictions considering Ontario-style automated enforcement bans
Historical price reaction to prior AI-lab news days to confirm sympathy-fade pattern

discoveries.name: nCino, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: Risk Factors are unusually candid about AI: 'Our growth strategy increasingly depends on the adoption and effective use of our AI capabilities' and competitors' AI/ML offerings could reduce demand. However, the moat is real: nCino is embedded in regulated bank workflows on Salesforce Platform, with FI examination, 3-5 year non-cancelable contracts, and asset-based pricing tied to bank balance sheets.
discoveries.thesis: An Anthropic regulatory ban is the opposite of a sympathy-fade catalyst for nCino — if anything, it signals AI deployment friction that protects incumbent regulated-workflow vendors. nCino's filings show genuine AI/ML competitive pressure, but a US government ban on a specific lab's models doesn't accelerate that pressure; it slows it. Multi-year subscription revenue recognition also means today's news cannot meaningfully change the next several quarters of revenue.
discoveries.what_confirms: Stable net retention and continued cross-sell into existing FI base; commercial onboarding solution traction; no incremental large-bank churn.
discoveries.what_kills: A major bank publicly switches from nCino to an LLM-based internal system, or a competitor announces a credible AI-native loan origination platform with regulatory blessing. The filings explicitly warn this is the long-term risk.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Vertical SaaS in regulated industries gets the WORST sympathy fades on AI lab news because the panic narrative is intuitive but the regulatory moat is invisible to retail. Confidence is only 3 because the filings DO acknowledge AI as a material competitive risk — this isn't a pure false victim.
discoveries.catalyst: US government bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 releases over security concerns
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: nCino is the canonical 'vertical SaaS gets eaten by AI' name retail trades on every AI-lab headline.
research_pointers: Quarterly net dollar retention rate trend
Adoption rate of nCino's own AI features (their growth thesis now depends on this)
Any disclosed FI customer evaluating internal LLM-based alternatives
Whether asset-based pricing is achieving net-positive ARR uplift

discoveries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K discusses AI as both opportunity and competitive risk but the core moat is explicit: 'substantially all of our revenues from sales of software and services to state, county, and city governments' with long sales cycles, open bidding processes, and multi-year contracts. AI risk language is forward-looking ('we may fail to enhance our offerings'), not present-tense revenue impairment.
discoveries.thesis: An Anthropic model ban has no mechanical pathway to Tyler revenue. Government procurement cycles run years; CIOs at the county-clerk level are not replacing the court-record system of record with an LLM, especially in a regulatory climate where the federal government just banned a frontier model on security grounds. The trigger arguably HELPS Tyler by reinforcing 'AI is risky for public sector' narratives. Elevated volume (2x) and a sharp drop on a thematic catalyst is the classic sympathy-fade signature.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued SaaS transition bookings; no major municipal RFP loss to an AI-native competitor; stable maintenance renewal rates.
discoveries.what_kills: A flagship state announces switching off a Tyler court or ERP system to an AI-native alternative. No evidence of this in filings or news.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Govtech is the cleanest false-victim pattern in AI sympathy fades — procurement cycles, FedRAMP, and political risk-aversion are deep moats invisible to retail. The 2x volume on a thematic-only catalyst is a textbook signal.
discoveries.catalyst: US government bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 releases over security concerns
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Tyler is the largest pure-play government vertical SaaS name and gets indexed into every AI-eats-software basket.
research_pointers: Check for any 8-K disclosure today or this week to rule out a non-AI catalyst
Convertible Senior Notes maturity March 15, 2026 — is settlement mechanics driving short interest?
Recurring revenue growth and SaaS mix trend
Track 2x volume signature against five_day pattern — already extended on the downside

skipped.reason: Up sharply on the day — not a sympathy-fade victim; AI capex beneficiary thesis is being expressed, not faded.
Up sharply on the day — market is treating edge-AI silicon as a beneficiary; no panic to calibrate.
Up sharply on the day — power-delivery for AI data centers trading as beneficiary; sympathy-fade does not apply.
Up sharply on the day — process-control for advanced nodes trading as AI capex beneficiary.

Modest drop, but filings show material non-AI overhangs (oncology cost trend, customer concentration with Molina/Centene, OBBBA Medicaid changes, Performance Suite risk). Move is not cleanly attributable to sympathy fade; real fundamental issues dominate the narrative.
